name: Native Pi agent vs pi-subagents actual evaluation

description: Fresh native agent vs pi-subagents actual eval baseline

asi.hypothesis: A fresh, isolated eval using source-backed evidence plus cheap tmux captures will show current native Pi has closed much of the old delegation/status/diagnostics gap while pi-subagents keeps async/control but has removed requested manager/status slash surfaces.
evidence: ./autoresearch.sh passed with actual_eval_score=251
captures/native-startup.txt and captures/subagents-startup.txt
source-probes.md current source evidence
scorecard.md filled 9 scenarios x 2 arms
findings.md scenario findings and limitations
isolation-proof.md booleans true

isolation_proof.native: Launched with --no-session --no-extensions --tools agent,read,grep,find,ls --thinking off; native captures used /agents, /agents-doctor, /agents-status only; no subagent tool/extension commands used.
isolation_proof.pi_subagents: Launched with --no-session --no-builtin-tools --no-extensions -e pi-subagents/src/extension/index.ts --thinking off; native built-in agent disabled; extension captures avoided /agents and native agent.

asi.task_agent_coverage: S09 source probe found no native action/taskId/create/list/get/update/delete lifecycle schema in packages/coding-agent/src/core/tools/agent.ts; pi-subagents management actions are not a general task-list equivalent.
key_findings: Native now has /agents-doctor, /agents-status, saved chains, and /agents run/parallel/run-chain scaffolds.
Installed pi-subagents is 0.24.0 and has removed /subagents manager UI and /subagents-status slash overlay.
Removed /subagents and /subagents-status probes fell through to parent model turns invoking extension subagent list, costing roughly 22k prompt and 187 completion tokens total.
No live child-agent outputs were run; source evidence used to avoid paid child runs.
asi.next_action_hint: If the next question is real output quality/token cost, run one symmetric low-cost live child scenario for S01-S03 under the same launch flags; otherwise prioritize implementing/verifying the native non-spawn task lifecycle surface for S09.
limitations: No live child-agent report quality measured.
Exact cache creation/read token accounting unavailable.
tmux captures prove command/UI surfaces but not deep selector semantics.

description: Add evidence manifest and integrity scoring

asi.hypothesis: The previous actual_eval_score was saturated on artifact presence; adding a real evidence manifest and scorer checks for scorecard evidence-file integrity should improve actual evaluation quality without padding or extra paid child-agent runs.
evidence: evidence-manifest.md maps all 18 scorecard rows to concrete evidence files and supporting live/source captures
./autoresearch.sh verifies every scorecard evidence file exists, manifest row coverage is complete, live capture links are present, and pi-subagents 0.24.0 removed-surface guards remain
run_experiment parsed actual_eval_score=307, evidence_file_coverage=18, evidence_manifest_rows=18, live_capture_links=8, version_guard_verified=1

isolation_proof.native: No native-arm scenario was rerun or changed to use pi-subagents; existing launch proof remains --no-extensions with native agent/read/grep/find/ls only.
isolation_proof.pi_subagents: No extension-arm scenario used native /agents or native agent; existing launch proof remains --no-builtin-tools --no-extensions -e pi-subagents.

asi.why_not_overfit: The new scorer dimension catches stale or missing evidence paths and removed-version drift; it would fail if scorecard rows cited nonexistent captures, so it rewards reproducibility rather than keyword padding.
asi.next_action_hint: Next most valuable iteration is either a single symmetric low-cost live child run for S01/S02 output quality, or rerun S09 if native task lifecycle code lands; avoid repeating removed-command fallthrough probes unless measuring UX cost.
limitations: No new child-agent model run; this iteration improves evidence integrity, not live output quality.
Metric formula changed within the same actual_eval_score goal to remove saturation; compare against prior score with that caveat.

description: Add token evidence for removed-command fallthrough cost

asi.hypothesis: The eval underrepresents token/value tradeoffs because removed-command fallthrough token/cost evidence is only buried in tmux captures; a token-evidence artifact plus scorer checks should make that behavior reproducible without new paid child-agent runs.
evidence: token-evidence.md records five live footer rows: three native registered commands at $0.000 and two pi-subagents removed-command fallthroughs
captures/subagents-s05-status-removed-live.txt shows ↑11k ↓106 $0.056 after /subagents-status fell through to model and invoked subagent list
captures/subagents-s07-manager-removed-live.txt shows ↑11k ↓81 $0.055 after /subagents fell through to model and invoked subagent list
run_experiment parsed actual_eval_score=345, token_evidence_rows=5, native_zero_cost_captures=3, removed_command_token_captures=2, fallthrough_cost_cents=11.1

isolation_proof.native: No native-arm rerun or extension tool use; token evidence reads existing native captures from launches with --no-extensions and native /agents* commands only.
isolation_proof.pi_subagents: No native agent/tool use; token evidence reads existing extension-arm captures from --no-builtin-tools --no-extensions -e pi-subagents launches.

asi.why_not_overfit: This adds an independently useful token/value artifact and validates real footer evidence from captures. It would fail if the cost lines disappeared or if native registered commands were not local zero-cost paths.
asi.next_action_hint: Next most valuable non-padding iteration is a lightweight automated consistency check between scorecard numeric scores and findings winners, or one symmetric live child-output run if token budget is acceptable.
limitations: Footer prompt tokens are rounded as ↑11k, so aggregate prompt token estimate is approximate.
No exact cache creation/read details; only visible footer tokens/cost are captured.
No new child-agent output quality measured.

description: Add scorecard consistency analysis and checks

asi.hypothesis: A scorecard consistency gate will improve the actual eval by deriving averages and numeric winners from the filled rows, catching stale conclusions instead of adding narrative padding.
evidence: scripts/check-scorecard-consistency.py parses all 18 scorecard rows and 108 numeric cells, enforces 1-5 score ranges, and validates summary averages
The check caught and fixed a real stale summary value: pi-subagents UX average was 3.2 but computed to 3.3
score-analysis.md records computed averages and numeric scenario winners: native=7, pi-subagents=2, tie=0
run_experiment parsed actual_eval_score=409 with scorecard_average_consistency=1 and scorecard_analysis_verified=1

isolation_proof.native: No native-arm scenario was rerun and no pi-subagents tooling was used; this iteration only computes from existing scorecard/captures under pi-agent-tool.
isolation_proof.pi_subagents: No native agent/tool use in the extension arm; this iteration only computes from existing scorecard/captures under pi-agent-tool.

asi.why_not_overfit: The gate is adversarial to the existing artifacts: it failed on the stale UX average before the scorecard was fixed, so it can catch real inconsistency rather than simply rewarding extra text.
asi.next_action_hint: Next best iteration is either a findings-to-score-analysis alignment check for qualitative winners, or a deliberately tiny symmetric live child run if output quality evidence becomes worth the token spend.
limitations: The numeric winner analysis reflects scorecard weights, not an independent human review.
S02/S03 remain prose capability ties despite native numeric edge, documented in score-analysis.md.
No new child-agent output quality measured.

asi.hypothesis: The eval can still drift if prose winners diverge from numeric scorecard winners without explicit justification; a findings-alignment gate should preserve honest qualitative judgment while failing undocumented conflicts.
evidence: scripts/check-findings-alignment.py parses score-analysis.md and findings.md, emits findings-alignment.md, and fails on conflicts
Initial script run exposed that headings needed scenario-id normalization; after fixing, it reports 9 rows, 5 aligned findings, 4 intentional exceptions, 0 conflicts
findings-alignment.md documents exceptions for S02/S03 capability ties, S06 diagnostics tie, and S09 no-current-winner despite pi-subagents closest-equivalent numeric edge
run_experiment parsed actual_eval_score=455 and findings_alignment_verified=1

isolation_proof.native: No native-arm scenario was rerun and no extension tooling was used; this iteration only analyzes existing eval artifacts.
isolation_proof.pi_subagents: No native agent/tool use in extension arm; this iteration only analyzes existing eval artifacts.

asi.why_not_overfit: The gate constrains the prose instead of rewarding more prose: undocumented differences now fail, while documented exceptions keep qualitative judgment visible. The first run failed with 9 conflicts before the parser was corrected, showing it is a real guard.
asi.next_action_hint: Next non-padding iteration could add a source/capture drift detector for pi-subagents command removals, or stop artifact-hardening and spend one tiny symmetric live child run if output quality evidence is required.
limitations: The allowed exceptions are curated judgment calls; changing score weights or final recommendation should revisit them.
No new live child-agent output quality measured.
The alignment check validates winner labels, not all prose claims.

asi.hypothesis: The eval's isolation and removed-command claims can drift as native Pi or pi-subagents changes; a source-backed command-surface detector should keep those claims reproducible without running more model turns.
evidence: scripts/check-command-surface.py parses native built-in slash commands, pi-subagents extension registerCommand calls, package version, launch flags, and changelog removals

command-surface.md records native /agents, /agents-doctor, /agents-status present; extension /run, /chain, /parallel, /run-chain, /subagents-doctor present; extension /agents, /subagents, /subagents-status absent

run_experiment parsed command_surface_native_expected_present=3, command_surface_extension_expected_present=5, command_surface_extension_removed_absent=3, command_surface_launch_isolation=2, command_surface_verified=1
actual_eval_score=528

isolation_proof.native: No native-arm scenario rerun; detector verifies existing native startup capture includes --no-extensions and native agent tool allowlist.
isolation_proof.pi_subagents: No native agent/tool use; detector verifies existing extension startup capture includes --no-builtin-tools, --no-extensions, and explicit pi-subagents -e path.

asi.why_not_overfit: This is a drift detector: if pi-subagents reintroduces /subagents or /subagents-status, or launch flags/source commands change, the scorer fails and forces the eval to update instead of preserving stale claims.
asi.next_action_hint: At this point artifact-hardening is strong; next valuable work is either a minimal symmetric live child output-quality run, or stop and finalize if avoiding model spend remains the priority.
limitations: It validates source/capture command surfaces, not an interactive slash completion list.
Native /agents still exists in the host during extension launches; the detector only asserts it is not an extension command and the eval avoids using it.
No new child-agent output quality measured.
